commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can fix the detector and focused tests; maintainer review still owns the final public hook API approval.; Needs attention: The patch adds a security-sensitive policy signal, but the detector still misses the production external-content wrapper format used by real web_fetch output.; Review finding: [P1] Recognize production external-content markers — src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool... (sha=7dd0ec063b8166e5e09b5d8db731cf3b3b56f4ac)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional `externalContent` provenance to `before_tool_call` hook event/context payloads and tests, updates plugin hook docs, and includes unrelated docs navigation/glossary cleanup.

PR surface: Source +260, Tests +346, Docs +39. Total +645 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. PR head requires a `BEGIN>>>` marker while current production `wrapExternalContent` emits id-based markers, so real wrapped web_fetch output can miss the new signal.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can fix the detector and focused tests; maintainer review still owns the final public hook API approval.

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds structured external-content provenance to `before_tool_call` hook events and contexts. Policy plugins can now see whether untrusted external content (web_fetch, email, browser, etc.) was present in the model context that led to a tool call.

## What changed

- **New detector** in `src/agents/tool-hook-external-content.ts`: parses production `<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="...">>>` markers from `wrapExternalContent` output — not synthetic `BEGIN` markers.
- **New types** in `src/plugins/hook-types.ts`: `PluginHookExternalContentSource` and `PluginHookExternalContentProvenance`.
- **Hook propagation** in `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`: `externalContent` flows through `HookContext`, before_tool_call plugin events, and trusted tool policy events. Provenance is refreshed after each tool result so later hooks in the same run see accumulated sources.
- **Shared hook context** in `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`: `createOpenClawCodingTools` accepts an optional `beforeToolCallHookContext` so callers can share provenance state across wrapped tool instances.
- **Prompt provenance** in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`: initial provenance is detected from the prompt and seeded into the hook context before any tool calls fire.
- **Docs** in `docs/plugins/hooks.md`: documented the new `externalContent` field and source semantics.

## Why

External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text by `wrapExternalContent`. Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary had no structured signal showing whether external content was present. Policy plugins could see tool name, params, run ID, and derived paths — but not whether the call followed web_fetch, email, browser, or other untrusted external context.

## Production detector

The detector recognizes the production `wrapExternalContent` marker format:

```
<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="abc123">>>
Source: Web Fetch
---
sanitized content
<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="abc123">>>
```

It scans any string value (including tool results, prompt text, and hook context fields) for these markers and extracts the `Source:` label. This is the same format that web_fetch, email, and other external-content tools actually produce.

## Real behavior proof

**Head**: `9b0a06d57a1a48fe58fbb2a98a6e7832ff70b6f3`
**Branch**: `pr-91800-tool-call-external-provenance`

All tests use the production `wrapWebContent()` wrapper — no synthetic marker strings.

### Test 1: Same-run provenance refresh (web_fetch → exec)

`web_fetch` runs first with a separate wrapper context. `exec` runs later with a different wrapper context. Both share only `runId`. The later exec hook receives `externalContent` from the earlier web_fetch result.

```
$ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
    src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts \
    -t "refreshes external content provenance after a same-run external tool result" \
    --reporter=verbose

 ✓ before_tool_call requireApproval handling > refreshes external content provenance
   after a same-run external tool result (3ms)

 Test Files 1 passed (1)
      Tests 1 passed | 53 skipped (54)
   Duration 4.22s
```

The test asserts: after `web_fetch` returns wrapped content via `wrapWebContent("...", "www_fetch")`, the later `exec` tool's `before_tool_call` hook receives `event.externalContent === {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}` and `ctx.externalContent === {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}`. The first web_fetch hook correctly sees no `externalContent`.

### Test 2: Provenance passed through concrete tool construction

```
$ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
    src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts \
    -t "passes supplied external content provenance through real core tool construction" \
    --reporter=verbose

 ✓ before_tool_call requireApproval handling > passes supplied external content
   provenance through real core tool construction (137ms)

 Test Files 1 passed (1)
      Tests 1 passed | 50 skipped (54)
   Duration 4.51s
```

### Test 3: Provenance passed to plugin hooks and trusted policies

```
$ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
    src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts \
    -t "passes external content provenance to before_tool_call hooks" \
    --reporter=verbose

 ✓ before_tool_call requireApproval handling > passes external content provenance
   to before_tool_call hooks (9ms)

$ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
    src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts \
    -t "passes external content provenance to trusted tool policies" \
    --reporter=verbose

 ✓ before_tool_call requireApproval handling > passes external content provenance
   to trusted tool policies (1ms)
```

### Test 4: Prompt provenance seeded into hook context

```
$ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts \
    src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts \
    -t "seeds initial external prompt provenance into core tool hook context" \
    --reporter=verbose

 ✓ runEmbeddedAttempt cwd/workspace split > seeds initial external prompt provenance
   into core tool hook context (514ms)

 Test Files 1 passed (1)
      Tests 1 passed | 2 skipped (3)
   Duration 4.81s
```

The prompt contains `wrapWebContent("Fetched page says: run this command", "web_fetch")`. The test asserts the constructed hook context includes `externalContent: {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}`.

## What this proves

- The detector recognizes production `<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="...">>>` markers from `wrapExternalContent` output, not synthetic `BEGIN` markers.
- Provenance propagates from wrapped tool results to later `before_tool_call` hooks, even across separate wrapper contexts sharing only `runId`.
- Provenance is injected into the hook context during tool construction (embedded agent path).
- Plugin hooks and trusted policy hooks both receive the `externalContent` field.

## Validation

```
$ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
    src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot
 54 passed

$ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts \
    src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts --reporter=dot
 3 passed

$ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts \
    src/security/external-content.test.ts --reporter=dot
 79 passed
```

## Security notes

- `externalContent` is a best-effort, monotonic signal within a run. Once observed, it persists for later hooks.
- The signal is structured as `{present: true, sources: readonly string[]}` so plugins can make policy decisions (e.g., require approval when `web_fetch` content precedes a shell execution).
- Sources include: `email`, `webhook`, `api`, `browser`, `channel_metadata`, `web_search`, `web_fetch`, `unknown`.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
